content.problemStatement: Given a sorted array of N integers and an integer x, write a program to find the upper bound of x.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers representing the sorted array. The third line contains an integer x, the target value.
content.outputFormat: The index of the upper bound of x in the array. If no such element exists, return N.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

code.Python3:
class UpperBoundFinder:
    # Linear search to find upper bound
    def upper_bound(self, arr, x):
        for i in range(len(arr)):
            if arr[i] > x:
                return i  # First element greater than x
        return len(arr)   # Return size if no such element found

# Driver code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
x = int(input())
finder = UpperBoundFinder()
ind = finder.upper_bound(arr, x)

print(ind)
